items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
items.standfirst: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.
items.content: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: US and Iran trade strikes again, after Trump warns Tehran will ‘pay the price’ for stalled talks
items.standfirst: US launches second round of airstrikes on Iran, and Tehran responds by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and JordanUS strikes Iran for second day, as ceasefire appears close to collapseThree Indian seafarers were killed in a US attack on an oil tanker earlier this week, India’s shipping minister, ‌Sarbananda Sonowal, said.“It is deeply unfortunate to learn of the tragic incident aboard the Palau-flagged MT Settebello. Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified,” he wrote in a post on X.The Middle East is being pulled deeper into crisis & the consequences reach far beyond the region.” Continue reading...
items.content: US launches second round of airstrikes on Iran, and Tehran responds by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and JordanUS strikes Iran for second day, as ceasefire appears close to collapseThree Indian seafarers were killed in a US attack on an oil tanker earlier this week, India’s shipping minister, ‌Sarbananda Sonowal, said.“It is deeply unfortunate to learn of the tragic incident aboard the Palau-flagged MT Settebello. Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified,” he wrote in a post on X.The Middle East is being pulled deeper into crisis & the consequences reach far beyond the region.” Continue reading...

items.title: Iran war day 104: Iran attacks US bases, closes strait after Trump strikes
items.standfirst: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, launches retaliatory strikes on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan after US attacks.
items.content: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, launches retaliatory strikes on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan after US attacks.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for second day, as ceasefire appears close to collapse
items.standfirst: Explosions reported across Iran after Donald Trump vowed to ‘hit them hard again’, with Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan targeted by TehranMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US launched a new round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after Donald Trump warned Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations, prompting Iran to respond with strikes targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.The new US assault across a range of Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end to the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the strait of Hormuz. The American attack appeared more intense and wider than the day before, but Iran released no information about what was hit. Continue reading...
items.content: Explosions reported across Iran after Donald Trump vowed to ‘hit them hard again’, with Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan targeted by TehranMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US launched a new round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after Donald Trump warned Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations, prompting Iran to respond with strikes targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.The new US assault across a range of Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end to the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the strait of Hormuz. The American attack appeared more intense and wider than the day before, but Iran released no information about what was hit. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran targets Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after new US strikes
items.standfirst: Iran remains defiant amid US attacks, orders complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels.
items.content: Iran remains defiant amid US attacks, orders complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels.

items.title: Middle East live: US launches new strikes on Iran as Tehran says Strait of Hormuz closed
items.standfirst: The United States launched a second round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations. Iran said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to US attacks and launched its own strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The United States launched a second round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations. Iran said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to US attacks and launched its own strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: U.S. launches ‘self defense’ strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter shot down
items.standfirst: The U.S. launched what it called “self-defense strikes” after an American helicopter was shot down by an Iranian drone over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump vowed Iran would “pay the price.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.
items.content: The U.S. launched what it called “self-defense strikes” after an American helicopter was shot down by an Iranian drone over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump vowed Iran would “pay the price.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

items.title: U.S. launches a second day of strikes on Iran and Iran fires back at Gulf States
items.standfirst: The new U.S. assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz
items.content: The new U.S. assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)

items.title: Trump says Iran has taken too long to negotiate, will 'pay the price'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. "Iran ‌is all talk and no action," Trump wrote in ⁠a social media post. "They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, ‌now they will have to pay the price!!!"
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. "Iran ‌is all talk and no action," Trump wrote in ⁠a social media post. "They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, ‌now they will have to pay the price!!!"

items.title: Somali World Cup referee denied U.S. entry, hailed as hero at home
items.standfirst: A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.
items.content: A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.(Image credit: Abuukar Mohamed Muhidin)

items.title: M23 fighters detaining, abusing thousands in DRC
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition, a new Human Rights Watch report uncovers mass forced recruitment and detention by the Rwanda-backed M23 in eastern DR Congo. Also, we head to Durban where thousands of foreign nationals driven from their homes during xenophobic protests remain outside the Department of Home Affairs. And he’s back on home soil to a thunderous welcome, but Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan’s World Cup dream has been cut short after he was denied entry to the United States.
items.content: In tonight's edition, a new Human Rights Watch report uncovers mass forced recruitment and detention by the Rwanda-backed M23 in eastern DR Congo. Also, we head to Durban where thousands of foreign nationals driven from their homes during xenophobic protests remain outside the Department of Home Affairs. And he’s back on home soil to a thunderous welcome, but Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan’s World Cup dream has been cut short after he was denied entry to the United States.

items.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia
items.standfirst: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.
items.content: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

items.title: US official claims banned referee had links with 'terror organisations'
items.standfirst: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations," says a US official.
items.content: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations," says a US official.

items.title: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup?
items.standfirst: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?
items.content: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?

items.title: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
items.standfirst: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".
items.content: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".

clusters.title: Knicks one win away from NBA title after record comeback against Spurs

items.title: Knicks one win away from NBA title after record comeback against Spurs
items.standfirst: New York Knicks rally to defeat the San Antonio Spurs by a point in a thrilling Game 4 of the NBA finals series.
items.content: New York Knicks rally to defeat the San Antonio Spurs by a point in a thrilling Game 4 of the NBA finals series.

items.title: Knicks rally from 29 points down and beat Spurs for 3-1 NBA Finals lead
items.standfirst: The New York Knicks made a record comeback from 29 points down and moved to the brink of their first championship since 1973 by beating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 on Wednesday night.
items.content: The New York Knicks made a record comeback from 29 points down and moved to the brink of their first championship since 1973 by beating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 on Wednesday night.(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)

items.title: Knicks rally from 29 points down to beat Spurs 107-106
items.standfirst: The Knicks set the record for the biggest NBA Finals comeback Wednesday night as they rallied from 29 points down to beat the Spurs 107-106.
items.content: The Knicks set the record for the biggest NBA Finals comeback Wednesday night as they rallied from 29 points down to beat the Spurs 107-106.

items.title: New York Knicks stage biggest comeback in NBA finals history
items.standfirst: The New York Knicks mounted the largest comeback in NBA Finals history on Wednesday, rallying from 29 points down to edge the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 and move within one win of the championship. OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds remaining sealed the victory, giving New York a 3-1 series lead.
items.content: The New York Knicks mounted the largest comeback in NBA Finals history on Wednesday, rallying from 29 points down to edge the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 and move within one win of the championship. OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds remaining sealed the victory, giving New York a 3-1 series lead.

items.title: French star Patrick Bruel charged with rape and sexual assault
items.standfirst: Singer and actor has denied all charges after more than 20 women made allegations against him dating back to 1990sThe French singer Patrick Bruel has been charged with rape and sexual assault in one of the biggest #MeToo cases in the French music industry.The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture, was placed under formal investigation over four cases that included alleged rape, attempted rape, sexual assault and sexual harassment. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape charges
items.standfirst: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.
items.content: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.

items.title: Has France failed its children? Outrage over murder of 11-year old Lyhanna
items.standfirst: Here in France, outrage. Outrage over what seems like the all-too-avoidable murder of 11-year old Lyhanna. The 41-year old suspect in her May 29th disappearance in a southwestern town - the father of a schoolmate - had twice previously been flagged and never questioned about suspected paedophile crimes. We’ll ask what went wrong and about a string of high profile cases that have recently shaken the nation, from the trial of a doctor in western France who abused patients over a period of decades and a new mayor of Paris - himself a past victim of child sexual abuse - who’s suspended dozens of after-school staff who were the subjects of complaints, to formal charges against French pop icon Patrick Bruel this Wednesday over alleged rape and abuse dating back to 1997.
items.content: Here in France, outrage. Outrage over what seems like the all-too-avoidable murder of 11-year old Lyhanna. The 41-year old suspect in her May 29th disappearance in a southwestern town - the father of a schoolmate - had twice previously been flagged and never questioned about suspected paedophile crimes. We’ll ask what went wrong and about a string of high profile cases that have recently shaken the nation, from the trial of a doctor in western France who abused patients over a period of decades and a new mayor of Paris - himself a past victim of child sexual abuse - who’s suspended dozens of after-school staff who were the subjects of complaints, to formal charges against French pop icon Patrick Bruel this Wednesday over alleged rape and abuse dating back to 1997.

items.title: France prosecutors want singer Bruel charged over alleged sexual abuse
items.standfirst: French prosecutors said Wednesday they had requested pop icon Patrick Bruel be charged and placed in pre-trial detention over nine allegations of sexual abuse including rape and sexual assault. The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture with multiple top selling albums and dozens of film appearances, has denied any wrongdoing. He is the latest top French celebrity to face investigators in the wake of the #MeToo movement, after film legend Gerard Depardieu was handed a suspended 18-month prison sentence last year for sexually assaulting two women on a film set. FRANCE 24's James André reports from the courthouse near Paris.
items.content: French prosecutors said Wednesday they had requested pop icon Patrick Bruel be charged and placed in pre-trial detention over nine allegations of sexual abuse including rape and sexual assault. The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture with multiple top selling albums and dozens of film appearances, has denied any wrongdoing. He is the latest top French celebrity to face investigators in the wake of the #MeToo movement, after film legend Gerard Depardieu was handed a suspended 18-month prison sentence last year for sexually assaulting two women on a film set. FRANCE 24's James André reports from the courthouse near Paris.

items.content: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline over charges it imposes on parents to sit next to their child.

items.title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with their children
items.standfirst: Budget airline describes inquiry as ‘bogus’ as watchdog says it is only large carrier flying from UK to impose chargeBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s biggest low-cost airline, Ryanair, is facing an investigation over the mandatory fee it charges a parent to sit with their child.The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said the Irish carrier’s terms and conditions require at least one parent to sit with their children, including those with disabilities, and bills them about £8 a flight to do so. Continue reading...
items.content: Budget airline describes inquiry as ‘bogus’ as watchdog says it is only large carrier flying from UK to impose chargeBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s biggest low-cost airline, Ryanair, is facing an investigation over the mandatory fee it charges a parent to sit with their child.The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said the Irish carrier’s terms and conditions require at least one parent to sit with their children, including those with disabilities, and bills them about £8 a flight to do so. Continue reading...
